name: Simplest Manifest - Single Image File
notes: Fully supported. Renders the image correctly using Mango's default ImageRenderer and OpenSeadragon.

name: Simplest Manifest - Audio
notes: Fully supported. Audio content is resolved and played using the custom AudioRenderer component.

name: Simplest Manifest - Video
notes: Fully supported. Video files are played using the custom HTML5 VideoRenderer with full controls.

name: Support Deep Viewing with Basic Use of a IIIF Image Service
notes: Fully supported. Integrates with standard IIIF Image API services (v2 and v3) via OpenSeadragon for multi-tile deep zoom.

name: Internationalization and Multi-language Values (label, summary, metadata, requiredStatement)
notes: Fully supported. Handles IIIF language maps, selecting the active locale (English, French, Spanish, Welsh) dynamically.

name: Simple Manifest - Book
notes: Fully supported. Displays sequential canvas thumbnails and permits navigating through multiple image canvases.

name: HTML in Annotations
notes: Fully supported. HTML annotations targeting canvases are parsed and rendered correctly as interactive overlays.

name: Simple Collection
notes: Mango resolves single manifests; viewing collections hierarchically is not supported.

name: CSS in an Annotation
notes: Fully supported. CSS rules and classes embedded in stylesheets are respected when rendering HTML annotations.

name: Missing Images in a Sequence
notes: Fully supported. Missing or corrupted images in the canvas strip are handled gracefully.

name: Addressing a Spatial Region
notes: Fully supported. Displays region-specific image fragments via OpenSeadragon clipping coordinates.

name: Grouping Annotations into Collections
notes: Fully supported. Handles nested AnnotationCollections referencing multiple pages.

name: Image in Annotations
notes: Fully supported. Renders static or service-based images inside HTML annotations.

name: Multiple Choice of Audio Formats in a Single View (Canvas)
notes: Fully supported. Resolves multiple audio formats in a single canvas and lists them as alternative choices or falls back gracefully to the primary supported source.

name: Embedding HTML in descriptive properties (label, summary, metadata, requiredStatement)
notes: Fully supported. Sanitizes and renders basic HTML formatting inside labels, summaries, and metadata lists.

name: Rights statement (rights, requiredStatement)
notes: Fully supported. Parses the rights field and displays the license details in the metadata panel.

name: Viewing direction and Its Effect on Navigation (viewingDirection)
notes: Mango parses viewingDirection (e.g. right-to-left), but manual layout toggling is required; it does not automatically re-orient layouts dynamically.

name: Book 'behavior' Variations (continuous, individuals) (behaviorimage)
notes: Supports individual page views, but continuous page layouts are basic.

name: Load a Preview Image Before the Main Content (placeholderCanvas)
notes: Parses placeholderCanvas and displays it during loading, but does not implement advanced multi-stage loading or crossfades.

name: Audio Presentation with Accompanying Image (accompanyingCanvas)
notes: Plays audio with the accompanying image displayed, but lacks tight synchronization for active sub-regions on the image.

name: Begin playback at a specific point - Time-based media (start)
notes: Fully supported. Respects the start property and begins playback of time-based media at the specified timestamp.

name: Metadata on any Resource (metadata)
notes: Fully supported. Correctly parses metadata fields attached directly to canvases or specific files.

name: Linking to Web Page of an Object (homepage)
notes: Fully supported. Homepage links are parsed and displayed within the metadata panel.

name: Linking to Structured Metadata (seeAlso)
notes: Fully supported. seeAlso metadata endpoints are resolved and linked in the details panel.

name: Image Thumbnail for Manifest (thumbnail)
notes: Fully supported. Resolves thumbnails for the manifest and displays them in search/header lists.

name: Displaying Multiple Values with Language Maps (label, summary, metadata, requiredStatement)
notes: Fully supported. Handles multiple localized entries and lets the user toggle active language displays.

name: Load Manifest Beginning with a Specific Canvas (start)
notes: Fully supported. Follows the start canvas index/id parameters.

name: Navigation by Chronology (navDate)
notes: Mango does not have a timeline navigator UI for chronologically sorting/browsing canvases.

name: Acknowledge Content Contributors (provider)
notes: Fully supported. Provider information is displayed under the metadata panel.

name: Table of Contents for Book Chapters (structures)
notes: Fully supported. Displays book structures/chapters hierarchically in the Table of Contents navigation tree.

name: Table of Contents for A/V Content
notes: Fully supported. Renders ranges as clickable video/audio chapters that seek to the specific start times during playback.

name: Alternative Page Sequences
notes: Multiple sequences are parsed, but the sequence browser interface is simple.

name: Multi-volume Work with Individually-bound Volumes
notes: Collections are currently not supported; only individual manifests can be loaded.

name: Multiple Volumes in a Single Bound Volume
notes: Fully supported. Handled via chapter/ranges pointing to canvases within the same manifest.

name: Multiple Choice of Images in a Single View (Canvas)
notes: Toggling between alternative image layers (the choice property) is not natively supported inside OpenSeadragon.

name: Foldouts, Flaps, and Maps (behavior)
notes: Renders these as separate canvases, but layout presets for overlapping foldouts or flaps are not automated.

name: Composition from Multiple Images
notes: Fully supported. OpenSeadragon supports tiling and composing multiple source images on a single canvas.

name: Adding Thumbnail Navigation and no-nav to a Video Resource
notes: Fully supported. Video thumbnail tracks are fully displayed, and controls obey behaviors.

name: Simplest Manifest - Single Image File
notes: Fully supported. Renders the image correctly using Mango's default ImageRenderer and OpenSeadragon.

name: Image and Canvas with Differing Dimensions
notes: Fully supported. OpenSeadragon automatically scales and fits image coordinates to differing canvas aspect ratios.

name: Support Deep Viewing with Basic Use of a IIIF Image Service
notes: Fully supported. Integrates with standard IIIF Image API services (v2 and v3) via OpenSeadragon for multi-tile deep zoom.

name: Simple Manifest - Book
notes: Fully supported. Displays sequential canvas thumbnails and permits navigating through multiple image canvases.

name: Viewing direction and Its Effect on Navigation (viewingDirection)
notes: Mango parses viewingDirection (e.g. right-to-left), but manual layout toggling is required; it does not automatically re-orient layouts dynamically.

name: Image Rotation Two Ways
notes: Fully supported. Supports 90/180/270 degree rotation services on canvas images.

name: Load Manifest Beginning with a Specific Canvas (start)
notes: Fully supported. Follows the start canvas index/id parameters.

name: Missing Images in a Sequence
notes: Fully supported. Missing or corrupted images in the canvas strip are handled gracefully.

name: Simplest Manifest - Audio
notes: Fully supported. Audio content is resolved and played using the custom AudioRenderer component.

name: Simplest Manifest - Video
notes: Fully supported. Video files are played using the custom HTML5 VideoRenderer with full controls.

name: Load a Preview Image Before the Main Content (placeholderCanvas)
notes: Parses placeholderCanvas and displays it during loading, but does not implement advanced multi-stage loading or crossfades.

name: Audio Presentation with Accompanying Image (accompanyingCanvas)
notes: Plays audio with the accompanying image displayed, but lacks tight synchronization for active sub-regions on the image.

name: Begin playback at a specific point - Time-based media (start)
notes: Fully supported. Respects the start property and begins playback of time-based media at the specified timestamp.

name: Providing Access to Transcript Files of A/V Content (rendering)
notes: Fully supported. Lists external transcription files or resource rendering formats in the details panel.

name: Table of Contents for A/V Content
notes: Fully supported. Renders ranges as clickable video/audio chapters that seek to the specific start times during playback.

name: Table of Contents for Multiple A/V Files on a Single Canvas (start)
notes: Plays multiple files sequentially, but sub-chapter duration mapping has minor sync issues.

name: Table of Contents for Multiple A/V Files on Multiple Canvases
notes: Fully supported. Connects structures to separate canvases and handles smooth transition during playback.

name: Using Caption and Subtitle Files in Multiple Languages with Video Content
notes: Fully supported. Integrates standard WebVTT tracks in different languages directly into the VideoRenderer.

name: Scholarly Annotation of a Poetry Reading
notes: Fully supported. Annotations with temporal targets are displayed in sync with audio playback.

name: Using Caption and Subtitle Files with Video Content
notes: Fully supported. Captions/subtitles are supported using standard track elements.

name: Multiple Choice of Audio Formats in a Single View (Canvas)
notes: Fully supported. Resolves multiple audio formats in a single canvas and lists them as alternative choices or falls back gracefully to the primary supported source.

name: Annotating a specific point of an image
notes: Fully supported. Renders HTML/SVG point markers precisely on the zoomable image canvas.

name: Simple Annotation — Tagging
notes: Fully supported. Text annotation tags are resolved and listed inside the annotations sidebar panel.

name: Redirecting from one Canvas to another resource (Hotspot linking)
notes: Hotspot annotation links targeting other canvases or external web resources do not automatically trigger redirection.

name: Tagging with an External Resource
notes: Fully supported. Resolves and links annotation tags to external RDF or Web pages.

name: Annotation with a Non-Rectangular Polygon
notes: Fully supported. Renders complex polygon paths over the OpenSeadragon viewport via SVG overlay.

name: Simplest Annotation
notes: Fully supported. Full canvas comment annotations are listed in the side drawer.

name: Embedded or referenced Annotations
notes: Fully supported. Resolves embedded items array or external AnnotationPage references.

name: Linking external Annotations targeting a Canvas to a Manifest
notes: Fully supported. Parses external annotations defined on the canvas level.

name: Annotate specific images or layers
notes: Fully supported. Allows target matching specific canvas media resources.

name: Annotating in Multiple Languages
notes: Fully supported. Selects appropriate language variants for annotation bodies.

name: Rendering Multiple Media Types on a Time-Based Canvas
notes: Audio and video play, but complex layout grids overlapping on a single timeline have minor presentation issues.

name: Sharing a link to open a Manifest in a specific viewer
notes: Fully supported. Content state loader supports query parameters for quick manifest sharing.

name: Open a specific region of a Canvas in a viewer
notes: Fully supported. Deep zoom viewport centers on the canvas region matching the target coordinate.

name: Sharing a link for opening two or more Canvases
notes: Opens the target canvases, but custom dual-panel layout setups are basic.

name: Drag and Drop
notes: Fully supported. Users can drag and drop IIIF manifest URLs/files directly to load them.

name: Represent Canvas Fragment as a Geographic Area in a Web Mapping Client
notes: Geographic coordinate parsing and map layer integrations are not supported.

name: Locate a Manifest on a Web Map
notes: Geographic location markers using navPlace are not displayed.

name: Locate Multiple Canvases on a Web Map
notes: Geographic metadata (navPlace on canvases) is not integrated with a web map.

name: Locating an Item in Place and Time
notes: Combined geographic (navPlace) and temporal (navDate) mapping views are not supported.
